question.text: As per Regulation 3(2A) of SEBI (PIT) Regulations, 2015, who is responsible for making a policy for determination of 'legitimate purposes'?

answer.text: The board of directors of the listed company

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Regulation 3(2A) states that the board of directors of a listed company shall make a policy for the determination of 'legitimate purposes' as part of the Codes of Fair Disclosure and Conduct under Regulation 8. **The Trap:** While the compliance officer administers the code, it is the board that must formulate the policy. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series III-B also covers board-level responsibilities in insider trading compliance. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Legitimate purposes include sharing UPSI with partners, lenders, merchant bankers, legal advisors, auditors etc. in the ordinary course of business.
